name: MCPToolkit
description: Personal tool library — portable across AI agents via MCP
author: XEXKX

tools.name: tool-forge
tools.description: Generates a new ready-to-use tool from a goal description. Produces tool.yaml + implementation + registers in manifest.

inputs.name: goal
inputs.description: What should the tool do? (natural language)

tools.outputs: New tool folder in tools/, registered in manifest.json, committed to git
examples: tool-forge --goal 'Stáhni všechny obrázky z URL a ulož je do složky' --runtime ps1
tool-forge --goal 'Analyzuj Python soubor a vrať seznam funkcí' --runtime py

tools.name: new-git-repo
tools.description: Initializes a local folder as a git repo, creates GitHub repository, sets up auto-push watcher and Windows Startup entry.

inputs.name: token
inputs.description: GitHub PAT (or set GITHUB_TOKEN env var)

inputs.name: skip_github
inputs.description: Local git only, skip GitHub API

tools.outputs: Git repo initialized, GitHub repo created, auto-push configured, startup entry added

tools.name: mcp-scout
tools.description: Finds, installs and registers external MCP servers from GitHub into MCPToolkit by category.

inputs.name: action
inputs.description: list | install | search — list categories, install by name, search by keyword

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: MCP server name to install (use with action=install)

inputs.name: category
inputs.description: Filter by category: code | system | browser | data | text | all

inputs.name: keyword
inputs.description: Keyword to search in catalog (use with action=search)

inputs.name: dry_run
inputs.description: Show what would be installed without actually installing

tools.outputs: MCP server cloned, pip/npm installed, registered in mcp.json and manifest.json
